entities.name: super IC
entities.context: Individual contributor with AI leverage combining domain expertise, taste, creative direction, and AI fluency

entities.name: super IC model
entities.context: Organizational model transitioning from large specialized teams to small generalist teams with AI agents

entities.name: AI agents
entities.context: Technology enabling small teams to replace larger specialized ones

entities.name: domain expertise
entities.context: Deep specialized knowledge; prerequisite skill for super IC status

entities.name: taste
entities.context: Qualitative judgment ability; distinguishing skill for super ICs

entities.name: creative direction
entities.context: Ability to guide and shape creative output; super IC prerequisite

entities.name: AI fluency
entities.context: Competence working with AI tools; required but insufficient alone for super IC

entities.name: change management
entities.context: Process challenge for organizations adopting super IC model

entities.name: cultural resistance
entities.context: Major barrier to organizational AI adoption and team restructuring

entities.name: AI ROI
entities.context: Metric for anchoring budget discussions instead of headcount multipliers

relationships.context: Deep domain expertise is prerequisite; without it, just unemployed
Qualitative judgment distinguishes super IC from displaced worker
Ability to guide output is essential super IC skill
Required skill but insufficient alone without other three
Model depends on AI agents enabling small generalist teams
Execution risk requires careful transition management
Cultural barriers are major obstacle to adoption
Budget framing shift from headcount to AI ROI enables model adoption
